summary: Merged tracking PR with broad matching edits across ifcoget, Kirby specials, game-mode, ground/stage, menu, library, and baselib files. The bot report credits +5580 matched bytes, 22 newly matched functions, and 5 additional unmatched-item improvements. The diff shows a mix of type/signature recovery, field-based access replacing raw pointer math, small motion-state and cast fixes, recovered local structs, and several explicit permuter/regalloc tactics such as PAD_STACK, temporary inline wrappers, redundant variables, and comments labeled 'Permuter slop' or 'FAKE MATCH'.

what_changed: Bot report states matched code rose to 57.52%, +0.14%, +5580 bytes, with 22 newly matched functions and 5 improvements in unmatched items.

ifcoget.c gained data/struct reshaping around un_803F9E08 and un_804A1F58, asset-name storage in the struct initializer, HSD_GObjGetHSDObj usage, adjusted HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 arguments, and several stack/control-flow shaping edits.

Kirby special files changed motion-state constants, dat_attrs access shape, object user-data reloads, callback writes through separate locals, and PAD_STACK/permuter slop locals to close small match gaps.
gm_1601/gmmain_lib/gm types changed a record field and accessor return type from signed to unsigned and replaced raw save-data offset math with gmMainLib_8015EDBC()->x114[index].
Ground/stage edits added or expanded structs for grCastle and grRCruise, changed fn_802010A4 to take CollData*, and replaced byte-offset access with field access.
Several small touched files are only evidenced by changed-file metadata and the bot report because the loaded diff excerpt is truncated.
smart_moves: Recovered typed access in gm_80163298 and fn_802010A4 rather than preserving raw byte/word pointer math.
Updated owning headers/prototypes where the body evidence changed signatures or return types, including gmMainLib_8015D7EC and fn_802010A4.
Kept questionable match-only constructs visibly labeled with TODO, FAKE MATCH, or Permuter slop comments, making curator/reviewer cleanup targets searchable.
Paired grRCruise.c field access changes with gr/types.h layout additions, so the recovered field usage has a declared structure rather than isolated casts.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function match exposes repeated raw offset access into a known game struct, introducing a narrow temporary struct or field can both improve readability and support matching.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grRCruise_Entry replaces entry + offsets in fn_802010A4
diff_excerpt: grCastleParams x12C replaces pointer math at offset 0x12C

decomp_lessons.lesson: Signature and signedness fixes should propagate through implementation, header, and backing struct field when a matching edit changes the recovered type.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gmMainLib_8015D7EC s16* to u16* in .c and .h
diff_excerpt: fn_802010A4 u8* to CollData* in .c and .h

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small one-line accessor inlines can be used as a matching tactic, but this PR's TODO comments show that such helpers may be provisional when direct GET_GROUND or field access does not match.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: inline dat_attrs accessor in ftKb_SpecialNPk.c
diff_excerpt: grMaterial TODO 'is this GET_GROUND? calling it directly didn't work'
diff_excerpt: grRCruise TODO 'is this GET_GROUND? calling it directly didn't work'

naming_conventions.convention: For newly recovered but still semantically weak layout fields, this PR keeps address-style placeholder names such as x00, x02, x04, x08, x12C, pad_3C, and entries rather than inventing rich semantics.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grCastleParams x12C with TODO about array size

naming_conventions.convention: Temporary match-only variables are named generically and commented when they are not semantic, e.g. new_var/new_var2 with 'Permuter slop'.
Recovered local role names continue common decomp shorthand such as fp, gp, gp2, jobj, gobj, wrapper, record, score, and index.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: multiple touched functions use fp/gp/gobj/jobj/wrapper/record/index

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was added narrowly in several functions to close stack/register-sensitive matches.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: PAD_STACK(8) in ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCD60, ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCF74, ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FD020, ftKb_SpecialNSk_80105E8C, ftKb_SpecialNSk_80105F3C, grCastle_801CE7E8, un_802FF498
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Loaded evidence includes bot match results but no per-function objdiff logs beyond the bot summary.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Direct HSD_GObjGetUserData or HSD_GObjGetHSDObj calls were sometimes used instead of convenience macros or direct field access to affect codegen.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCD60 and grpstadium use HSD_GObjGetUserData
diff_excerpt: ftKb_SpecialNZd reloads fp through HSD_GObjGetUserData
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: PR evidence does not prove this as general style; it appears as match-specific codegen steering.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Local aliasing and separate pointer locals were introduced to influence register allocation.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: fp2 introduced in ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCF74 and ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FD020
diff_excerpt: wrapper copied to new_var in fn_801D3084
diff_excerpt: comment 'FAKE MATCH: redundant NULL checks required for regalloc'
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This is a tactic, not an accepted naming or style standard.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Typed field recovery replaced casts and byte offsets where a plausible struct was added or already existed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Some newly added layouts still carry TODOs or placeholder fields.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: For ifcoget, data layout was adjusted by adding literal strings into a static struct initializer and using a hard-coded memzero size with a TODO because sizeof was not yet matching.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: TODO says sizeof(un_804A1F58) should be 0x80; memzero uses 0x80
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This is high-risk data/layout work and should remain a curator/reviewer follow-up rather than a general lesson.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Propagate recovered signedness and pointer types through headers and structs
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2285 changed gmMainLib_8015D7EC from s16* to u16* in both implementation and header and changed FighterData.x7E from s16 to u16; it also changed fn_802010A4 from u8* to CollData* in both source and header.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer recovered field access over raw offset math when a plausible layout is available
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2285 replaced raw offset access in gm_80163298, grCastle_801CE7E8, and fn_802010A4 with typed fields such as x114[index], x12C[index], CollData flags, and grRCruise_Entry fields.

source_update_candidates.title: grRCruise ground vars include an entries pointer at +0x6C/+gp+0x130 in PR 2285 evidence
source_update_candidates.text: The PR added struct grRCruise_Entry and grRCruise_GroundVars.entries, then used gp->gv.rcruise.entries[i] in fn_802010A4. Candidate only; verify against current source/symbol metadata before promoting.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential durable layout fact, but PR evidence alone does not prove final field semantics or later refinements.

source_update_candidates.title: gmMainLib_8015D7EC and FighterData.x7E unsigned candidate
source_update_candidates.text: The PR changed gmMainLib_8015D7EC to return u16* and changed FighterData.x7E to u16, with gm_80163298 comparing and writing unsigned scores. Candidate only; verify against current headers and usage before promoting.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gm_80163298 uses u16* record and u16 score
source_update_candidates.reason: Likely type fact from implementation/header/struct consistency, but should be checked against current source and any later history.

source_update_candidates.title: ifcoget un_804A1F58 size/layout TODO follow-up
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2285 contains TODOs that sizeof(un_804A1F58) should be 0x80 and memzero currently uses hard-coded 0x80 until the struct size is fixed. Curator may want a cleanup/search task, not a standard.
source_update_candidates.reason: Explicit TODO in PR evidence; not suitable as accepted decomp practice.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not normalize visible 'Permuter slop' into accepted style
source_update_candidates.text: This PR used temporary locals, PAD_STACK, and direct HSD_GObjGetUserData calls to achieve matches, with comments explicitly labeling some as 'Permuter slop' or 'FAKE MATCH'. Existing standards already cover evidence requirements for tactics; this PR can be an example if the curator wants more examples, not a new rule.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: 'FAKE MATCH: redundant NULL checks required for regalloc'
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful as a cautionary example; do not promote match-only slop as source style.
